1. Document Control  
   Purpose: To establish the formal control framework for the document.  
   Expected content: Title, versioning, approval authority, and revision history.  
   Sample elements:  
   1.1 Document Title: Automated BI Validation Rules  
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft  
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council  
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author  

2. Executive Summary  
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.  
   Expected content: Summary of the action, its importance, and expected outcomes.  
   Sample elements: This document outlines the procedures for automating semantic validation, lineage tracking, and quality monitoring within the DWBI domain, aimed at enhancing data integrity and governance compliance.  

3. Domain and Context Framing  
   Purpose: To define the operational environment and maturity level of the governance initiative.  
   Expected content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning.  
   Sample elements:  
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Warehouse and Business Intelligence (DWBI)  
   3.2 Organizational Context: Overview of the organization’s data governance landscape.  
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 4 - Controlled  

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clarify the specific action being undertaken.  
   Expected content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DWBI-06, Automate semantic validation, lineage tracking, and quality monitoring  
   4.2 Action Objective: To establish automated BI validation rules that ensure data quality and compliance.  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applies to all BI reporting processes and associated data sources.  

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To detail the step-by-step execution of the action.  
   Expected content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and execution structure.  
   Sample elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To create a robust automated BI validation rule set.  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires a KPI set.  
   5.3 Deliverables: Automated BI rule set.  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Assemble a cross-functional team.  
   - Review master data and reference lists.  
   - Validate metric definitions against governed logic.  
   - Document validation rules using DAMA artefact practices.  
   - Conduct a review session with the Data Governance Council.  
   - Implement automated checks in BI tools.  
   - Perform a CMMI automation readiness assessment.  
   - Deploy the automated BI rule set.  

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To outline the governance framework supporting the action.  
   Expected content: Coverage, themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments utilizing BI reporting.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data sources feeding into BI systems.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, compliance, and automation.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office, Data Owners, Data Stewards.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles and responsibilities for decision-making.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Establish validation rules.  
   - Monitor compliance with governance policies.  
   - Review and update BI validation rules regularly.  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Quality and Data Architecture governance.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes non-BI related data processes.  

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms  
   Purpose: To define the mechanisms for monitoring and compliance.  
   Expected content: Monitoring, compliance alignment, and escalation model.  
   Sample elements:  
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs: Establish KPIs for data quality metrics.  
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: Ensure alignment with internal audit functions for compliance verification.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Define accountability timelines for addressing compliance issues.  
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization: (Only applicable if Score = 5)  

8. Document Governance  
   Purpose: To outline the management and ownership of the document.  
   Expected content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership.  
   Sample elements:  
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document will be reviewed annually.  
   8.2 Review Cycle: Scheduled reviews every six months.  
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Owned by the Data Governance Office.  

9. Appendices  
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.  
   Expected content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.  
   Sample elements:  
   9.1 Templates: BI validation rule template.  
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.  
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Previous audit reports and compliance checklists.
10. Evidence Required
- Approved document with version control
- Formal approval record
- RACI matrix attached
- Repository registration ID
- Stakeholder validation evidence

11. Acceptance Criteria
- Document formally approved
- Scope clearly defined
- Roles validated
- Deliverables stored in official repository
- Communication recorded

12. Control Owner
- Primary Owner: Domain Owner or Data Governance Office
- Oversight Authority: Data Governance Council
- Escalation Path: Chief Data Officer

13. Performance Metrics
- % of domains with approved artifacts
- % of reviews within SLA
- % of procedures with valid evidence
- Average update cycle time
- Non-compliance incidents per cycle

14. Monitoring Dashboard
- Domain compliance status
- Pending review alerts
- Governance heatmap
- KPI performance summary

15. Compliance Validation Cycle
- Mandatory semiannual review
- Annual internal audit
- Cross-domain validation
- RACI revalidation
- Escalation protocol

   Expected content: Title, version number, status, approval authority, and revision history.  
   Sample elements:  
   1.1 Document Title: Data Governance Artifact for BI and Warehousing Scope  
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0 - Draft  
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council  
   1.4 Revision History: Date, description of changes, author.

2. Executive Summary  
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.  
   Expected content: Summary of the action, procedure, and governance implications.  
   Sample elements: This document outlines the procedure for defining the business intelligence and warehousing vision, scope, and rules, ensuring alignment with organizational strategic goals.

3. Domain and Context Framing  
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance action.  
   Expected content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning.  
   Sample elements:  
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Warehousing and Business Intelligence (DWBI)  
   3.2 Organizational Context: Relevant departments include sales, finance, operations, and customer service.  
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent governance structure.

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clearly define the action to be taken within the governance framework.  
   Expected content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DWBI-01 - Define business intelligence and warehousing vision, scope, and rules  
   4.2 Action Objective: To establish a comprehensive BI business scope map.  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Focus on analytical subjects relevant to strategic goals.

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To outline the step-by-step execution of the defined action.  
   Expected content: Objectives, prerequisites, deliverables, and detailed execution steps.  
   Sample elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To define the BI and warehousing business scope.  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires BI vision.  
   5.3 Deliverables: BI business scope map.  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Assemble a cross-functional team of stakeholders.  
   - Conduct workshops to identify primary analytical subjects.  
   - Map business questions to master and reference domains.  
   - Define quality thresholds for data reliability.  
   - Capture the business scope using DAMA documentation practices.  
   - Incorporate CMMI scoping practices for delivery increments.  
   - Assign data stewardship roles and responsibilities.  
   - Schedule regular checkpoints for governance.

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To define the governance framework and its components.  
   Expected content: Coverage, themes, organizational structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: Stakeholders from sales, finance, operations, and customer service.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: Data sources relevant to BI initiatives.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, compliance, and analytical reliability.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council and Data Governance Office.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Data Owner for each analytical subject area.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Define roles and responsibilities.  
   - Schedule regular governance meetings.  
   - Review and update BI business scope map.  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with other governance domains.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Areas not covered by this governance action.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms  
   Purpose: To establish monitoring and compliance mechanisms for the governance action.  
   Expected content: Monitoring, compliance, audit alignment, and escalation pathways.  
   Sample elements:  
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs: No measurable KPIs due to Score 0.  
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: Conceptual compliance with governance policies.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Structural intent to define escalation pathways for governance issues.  
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization: Not applicable as Score is not 5.

8. Document Governance  
   Purpose: To outline the management and ownership of the document.  
   Expected content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and document ownership.  
   Sample elements:  
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.  
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Council.  
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office.

9. Appendices  
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.  
   Expected content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.  
   Sample elements:  
   9.1 Templates: BI business scope map template.  
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.  
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Previous BI initiative documentation.

   Purpose: To clearly articulate the action being defined and its parameters.

   - Validate business rules and metric definitions.  
   - Define roles and responsibilities.

   - Document approval outcomes.

   Purpose: To outline the governance framework and its components relevant to the action.

   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Office.

2. Executive Summary

   Sample Elements: This document outlines the updated Business Intelligence standards aimed at enhancing analytical and semantic governance through continuous improvement principles.

3. Domain and Context Framing  
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the document within the organizational framework.  
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and current maturity positioning.  
   Sample Elements:

   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 5 - Optimized

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being addressed by the document.  
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample Elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DWBI-09 - Continuously improve analytical and semantic governance using evidence  
   4.2 Action Objective: To enhance BI standards and lifecycle guidance through evidence-based updates.  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applicable to all BI standards and practices within the organization.

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To detail the procedural steps necessary for achieving the action objectives.  
   Expected Content: Objectives, prerequisites, deliverables, and a step-by-step execution structure.  
   Sample Elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To improve BI standards and lifecycle guidance.  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires governance insights.  
   5.3 Deliverables: Updated BI standards document.  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Gather Governance Insights  
   - Review and Update Modeling Rules  
   - Refine Metric Definitions and Metadata Requirements  
   - Clarify Source-of-Truth and Lineage Documentation  
   - Ensure Consistency Across Dimensions  
   - Validate Updates Through Governance Forums  
   - Finalize and Document Updated BI Standards  
   - Distribute and Communicate Updates  
   - Establish Continuous Improvement Mechanism

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To outline the governance framework that supports the execution of the procedure.  
   Expected Content: Coverage of organizational and data assets, governance themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample Elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments involved in BI initiatives.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets utilized in BI reporting.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, data lineage, and metadata management.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office, Data Owners, Data Stewards, Data Custodians.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles and responsibilities for decision-making.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Regular review of BI standards  
   - Stakeholder engagement for updates  
   - Documentation of governance insights  
   - Validation of updates through governance forums  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Quality and Data Architecture governance.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes non-BI related data governance activities.

   Purpose: To establish the monitoring and compliance framework for the procedure.  
   Expected Content: Monitoring mechanisms, compliance alignment, and escalation pathways.

   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: Ensure alignment with internal audit functions for compliance verification.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Define escalation pathways for unresolved issues related to BI standards.  
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization: Document evidence of continuous improvement initiatives and their impact on business value.

   Purpose: To outline the management and review processes for the document itself.
